profile.name: William
profile.role_in_household: Single household head; primary earner and sole decision-maker
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands basic investing and retirement accounts, but tends to focus more on cash flow and debt pressure than portfolio construction details
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Conservative in practice—prefers stability and predictability, gets uneasy with market swings, and is more motivated by avoiding losses and keeping bills covered than chasing higher returns
profile.communication_style: Direct but a bit guarded at first; uses qualifiers like "I think" and "probably" when recalling details; asks practical questions ("what do I do next month?") and may jump between topics when stressed
emotional_tendencies: anxious when discussing debt and monthly payments
profile.decision_making_style: Needs a clear recommendation with a short list of options; will decide once he sees how it affects monthly cash flow and reduces uncertainty
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to engage if the advisor is transparent on fees, avoids judgment, and shows quick wins on budgeting/debt strategy
internal_biases: recency bias (recent high bills feel like the new normal)
mental accounting (separates "retirement money" from "debt money" even when tradeoffs matter)
hidden_concerns: Worries that his debt situation will be judged or that he "should have known better"
Concerned that a conservative approach won’t be enough to reach retirement goals, but is afraid to take more risk
Health-related worry in the background that makes long-term planning feel urgent but also overwhelming
